title: <div>&quotI believe in the power of prayer, and it has been uplifting to hear th
content:
"I believe in the power of prayer, and it has been uplifting to hear that so many people are praying for me." -Amy Coney Barrett

Not one prayer -- not even all the prayers shouted simultaneously -- stopped any bullet fired in any school after the last bullet left the Columbine killer's gun and the whole world offered up prayers collectively to the victims of every single school shooting thus. So either prayer is worthless, or god wants the kids to die? Sorry if you picked two.

The aforementioned quote of the prospective employee are the exact words of someone who should absolutely not be sitting on the Supreme Court (a job requiring logic and critical thinking [not fucking faith]). Just like (I imagine) you’d love to still say to gay couples (and could very well revoke a previously established law to do so): Leave it at home. Keep your weird voodoo magic virgin births and ghost stories locked in the attic with all the other mythologies.

SEE!...I think you're dumb but I don't want to make a law that stops you from being dumb. That's the difference. The very, very, very, very, very, very, very...very important difference.
